---
title: "Probability Theory II: Martingales, Brownian Motion, and Stochastic Calculus Foundations"
subtitle: "Core Mathematics — Year 1"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Probability Theory II: Martingales, Brownian Motion, and Stochastic Calculus Foundations

**Track**: Core Mathematics — Year 1

Advanced probability covering martingale theory, Brownian motion, and the foundations of stochastic calculus. Essential for stochastic modeling in every domain track.

## Table of Contents

1. Conditional Expectation: Advanced Properties
2. Martingale Theory
3. Construction of Brownian Motion
4. Continuous-Time Martingales and Stochastic Integration Preview
5. Central Limit Theorem: Extensions and Refinements
6. Convergence of Random Series and Tail Events
7. Weak Convergence and Empirical Processes
8. Large Deviations

---

## Chapter 1: Conditional Expectation: Advanced Properties

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: Martingale Theory

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Construction of Brownian Motion

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Continuous-Time Martingales and Stochastic Integration Preview

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Central Limit Theorem: Extensions and Refinements

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Convergence of Random Series and Tail Events

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Weak Convergence and Empirical Processes

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Large Deviations

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
